question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 5.00% on 2026-04-11 for the Metaculus question "Will the United States gain formal sovereignty over any part of Greenland during 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on "Will the US gain formal sovereignty over Greenland during 2026?" will be strictly above 5.00% on April 11. The current CP is exactly 5.00%, right at the threshold boundary. Historical weekly snapshots show the CP oscillating between 4.0-5.0% over 6+ weeks, never once exceeding 5.00%. The historical mean (~4.48%) creates downward mean-reversion pressure. The underlying Greenland crisis has significantly de-escalated since January 2026 when Trump reversed on military force and tariff threats. No major catalysts are expected in the next 11 days. A simple random walk model gives ~50% from the threshold, but mean-reversion models give ~35-40%, and qualitative factors (no catalysts, crisis de-escalation, CP never above 5%) push toward ~30-35%.

factors.description: CP has never been observed above 5.00% in 6 weekly snapshots (all at 4.0-5.0%), suggesting 5% is a ceiling for this question
Historical mean CP (~4.48%) is below current 5.00%, creating downward mean-reversion pressure over the next 2 weeks
Greenland crisis de-escalated: Trump reversed on force and tariffs in Jan 2026, 'framework deal' refers to pre-existing 1951 treaty, no new provocations since
Denmark and Greenland maintain sovereignty as absolute red line; 85% of Greenlanders oppose US takeover; bipartisan US Congressional opposition
CP is exactly at threshold boundary — random fluctuation alone gives ~50% chance of crossing from this position (small-sample uncertainty)
Manifold markets at 7-11% on related questions (higher than Metaculus 5%), suggesting some forecasters could rationally update upward
Trump's unpredictability — could re-raise Greenland issue at any point, providing a catalyst for upward CP movement

reasoning:
**Forecast: 33% probability (YES)**

Key reasoning:
- The CP is exactly at the 5.00% threshold — a knife-edge situation
- In 6 weekly snapshots, the CP has **never** been above 5.00% (range: 4.0-5.0%)
- Historical mean (~4.48%) creates downward mean-reversion pressure
- The Greenland crisis has de-escalated significantly since January 2026 (Trump reversed on force/tariffs)
- No obvious catalysts in the next 11 days to push forecasters upward
- Mean-reversion Monte Carlo models give 35-40%; qualitative factors push slightly lower
- Main upside risk: Trump unpredictably re-raises the issue, or random fluctuation nudges the weighted median just above 5%

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41502) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-11 07:08:40 is higher than 5.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 5.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-11 07:08:40, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
